title: hieracium macilentum subsp. fariniceps
intro: Hieracium macilentum subsp. fariniceps is a subspecies of the Hieracium macilentum plant, commonly known as a type of hawkweed, characterized by its small stature and delicate appearance. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its high content of sesquiterpene lactones and flavonoids. The health benefits of Hieracium macilentum subsp. fariniceps include its potential to reduce inflammation and improve wound healing, making it a valuable remedy for skin conditions and infections. In herbal preparations, Hieracium macilentum subsp. fariniceps is typically dried and used as a tea, tincture, or infused in oil to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
